ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-17-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court stated that it has the memorandum regarding 7604 & 8547, that it is the court's position that 7604 is for an emergency only, that 8547 is controlling and that compensation shall not be done until the close of administration. Mr. Weinkauf provided a proposed order. The Court asked if proof of payment has been provided for filings of $450.00 each on 5/23/16 & 6/10/16 and $51.00 for certified copies of letters. Mr. Weinkauf responded that he will find out and respond. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-25-2016 Continued To Jun-08-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-27-2016 Continued To May-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Espey for (petitioner) Angelina Richardson.

The Court stated that this is a petition for probate, that there is question regarding proving the will, that the witnesses are not available and asked if adequate efforts have been made to find the heirs. Ms. Espey stated that the acknowledgment of signatures pointed out that it is the mother of her client, that they have located writing samples, that they can try and find others to verify the signature, that the decedent was a hoarder and very private, that her client and the witnesses were longtime friends, that in regards to the search for heirs the name of the decedent and his brother are common, that the Public Administrator had started a search, that the last known address for the brother was in 1940, that she did a search in the local directories narrowing things down with the age range, that they contacted those with contact information, that it was not conclusive and that it is possible that a professional would be able to find someone, possibly nieces and nephews. The Court stated that it knows there are relatives, that the question is who and where and that it would be more comfortable if a professional tried. Ms. Espey stated that her client is currently appointed as Special Administrator, that the letters expire today and asked for an extension. The court stated that it was a mistake to issue it with general powers but they are now past that problem and requested that Ms. Espey submit an order for the extension. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary was continued to May 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the issue of authenticating and proving the will and an heir search.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
